to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert

a lamentation

blithesomeness or glee, (religious or festival)

tokens.range: hating; an adversary

initiation, i.e. consecration
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes
to be high actively, to rise or raise (in various applications, literally or figuratively)
a song; abstractly, singing
probably to brighten up, i.e. (figuratively) be (causatively, make) blithe or gleesome

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

1.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe

tokens.text: praise to thee and not be silent
tokens.range: to be dumb; by implication, to be astonished, to stop; also to perish
play upon it; to make music, accompanied by the voice; hence to celebrate in song and music
physically, to throw (a stone, an arrow) at or away; especially to revere or worship (with extended hands); intensively, to bemoan (by wringing the hands)
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God

tokens.text: To the end that my glory
tokens.range: properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness
properly, concealed, i.e. the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e. (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial (especially with prepositional prefix) always

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.
Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.

properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo (for help, i.e. freedom from some trouble)

יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

a pit (especially as a trap); figuratively, destruction

נֶפֶשׁ — "soul" (Greek-influenced: non-material self) vs. "life" (the whole living person) vs. "self" (the person as subject). Hebrew nefesh is embodied, not a separate immaterial substance.
